concerns.text: FR-007 mandates the pipeline complete within '6 hours on a CPU-only environment with ≤ 7 GB RAM'. This is unverifiable in the current spec because the 'CPU-only environment' is not defined (e.g., specific CPU model, clock speed, core count). Without a defined hardware baseline, a test cannot deterministically pass or fail.

FR-002 and SC-002 require 'tSNR is ≥ 50 for ≥ 90% of voxels'. The spec does not define the method or formula for calculating tSNR (e.g., mean signal / std dev of noise, specific time-window exclusion). Different neuroimaging toolboxes (FSL vs. AFNI) may calculate this differently, making the requirement non-deterministic without a specified calculation method.

FR-004 requires the system to 'explicitly framing results as associational'. This is a semantic/narrative requirement that is not verifiable by a deterministic test or a reproducible procedure. A test cannot confirm 'framing' without subjective human judgment. This should be moved to a documentation requirement or a specific output string check (e.g., 'output text MUST contain the phrase...').

The edge case 'RAM usage stays below a defined threshold' is unverifiable because the threshold is not defined in the text. It references 'a defined threshold' but never states the value (e.g., 7GB). A test cannot be written against an undefined variable.
